Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public access
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket blocks public access at the bucket level. The control fails if any of the following settings are set to false: ignorePublicAcls, blockPublicPolicy, blockPublicAcls, restrictPublicBuckets.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: AWS account should be part of an AWS Organizations organization
Findings.Description: This control checks if an AWS account is part of an AWS Organizations organization. The control fails if the account is not part of an organization.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should use cross-Region replication
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has cross-Region replication enabled. The control fails if the bucket doesn't have cross-Region replication enabled.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

ProductFields.aws/securityhub/annotation: S3 bucket does not have a replication rule.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have Object Lock enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has Object Lock enabled. The control fails if Object Lock isn’t enabled for the bucket. If you provide a custom value for the 'mode' parameter, the control passes only if S3 Object Lock uses the specified retention mode.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

ProductFields.aws/securityhub/annotation: S3 default lock is not enabled

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have Lifecycle configurations
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has a Lifecycle configuration. The control fails if the bucket doesn't have a Lifecycle configuration. You can provide custom values for one or more if the preceding parameters, the control passes only if the policy includes the specified storage class, deletion time, and transition time.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

ProductFields.aws/securityhub/annotation: Found no bucket lifecycle configuration rules.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: SNS topics should be encrypted at-rest using AWS KMS
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an SNS topic is encrypted at rest using AWS KMS. The controls fails if an SNS topic doesn't use a KMS key for server-side encryption (SSE).

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

ProductFields.aws/securityhub/annotation: Amazon SNS Topic is not encrypted with KMS

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: ACLs should not be used to manage user access to S3 general purpose buckets
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket provides user permissions with an access control list (ACL). The control fails if an ACL is configured for managing user access on the bucket.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Effects/Data Exposure
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public write access
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public write access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public write access.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Effects/Data Exposure
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose bucket policies should restrict access to other AWS accounts
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket policy prevents principals from other AWS accounts from performing denied actions on resources in the S3 bucket. The control fails if the bucket policy allows one or more of the preceding actions for a principal in another AWS account.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Effects/Data Exposure
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should block public read access
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket permits public read access. It evaluates the block public access settings, the bucket policy, and the bucket access control list (ACL). The control fails if the bucket permits public read access.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Effects/Data Exposure
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: CloudWatch log groups should be retained for a specified time period
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Amazon CloudWatch log group has a retention period of at least the specified number of days. The control fails if the retention period is less than the specified number. Unless you provide a custom parameter value for the retention period, Security Hub uses a default value of 365 days.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have MFA delete enabled

Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have MFA delete enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether multi-factor authentication (MFA) delete is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if MFA delete is not enabled for the bucket. The control doesn't produce findings for buckets that have a lifecycle configuration.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have server access logging enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether server access logging is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if server access logging isn't enabled. When logging is enabled, Amazon S3 delivers access logs for a source bucket to a chosen target bucket. The target bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the source bucket and must not have a default retention period configured. The target logging bucket does not need to have server access logging enabled, and you should suppress findings for this bucket.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: VPCs should be configured with an interface endpoint for Docker Registry
Findings.Description: This control checks whether a virtual private cloud (VPC) that you manage has an interface VPC endpoint for Docker Registry. The control fails if the VPC doesn't have an interface VPC endpoint for Docker Registry. This control evaluates resources in a single account.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

ProductFields.aws/securityhub/annotation: This VPC does not use a VPC endpoint for service ecr.dkr.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: S3 general purpose buckets should have event notifications enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether S3 Event Notifications are enabled on an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if S3 Event Notifications are not enabled on a bucket. If you provide custom values for the 'eventTypes' parameter, the control passes only if event notifications are enabled for the specified types of events.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: Amazon EMR block public access setting should be enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether your account is configured with Amazon EMR block public access. The control fails if the block public access setting is not enabled or if any port other than 22 is allowed.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: IAM users' access keys should be rotated every 90 days or less
Findings.Description: This AWS control checks whether the active access keys are rotated within 90 days.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

StatusReasons.Description: AWS Config isn't recording all resource types that correspond to this control. Turn on recording for the following resources: AWS::IAM::User. To see all missing resource types for every enabled control, check your Config.1 findings.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: Unused IAM user credentials should be removed
Findings.Description: This control checks whether your IAM users have passwords or active access keys that were not used within the previous 90 days.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

StatusReasons.Description: AWS Config isn't recording all resource types that correspond to this control. Turn on recording for the following resources: AWS::IAM::User. To see all missing resource types for every enabled control, check your Config.1 findings.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: IAM policies should not allow full "*" administrative privileges
Findings.Description: This AWS control checks whether the default version of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies (also known as customer managed policies) do not have administrator access with a statement that has "Effect": "Allow" with "Action": "*" over "Resource": "*". It only checks for the Customer Managed Policies that you created, but not inline and AWS Managed Policies.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

StatusReasons.Description: AWS Config isn't recording all resource types that correspond to this control. Turn on recording for the following resources: AWS::IAM::Policy. To see all missing resource types for every enabled control, check your Config.1 findings.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: MFA should be enabled for all IAM users that have a console password
Findings.Description: This AWS control checks whether AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for all AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that use a console password.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

StatusReasons.Description: AWS Config isn't recording all resource types that correspond to this control. Turn on recording for the following resources: AWS::IAM::User. To see all missing resource types for every enabled control, check your Config.1 findings.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: IAM users should not have IAM policies attached
Findings.Description: This AWS control checks that none of your IAM users have policies attached. Instead, IAM users must inherit permissions from IAM groups or roles.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

StatusReasons.Description: AWS Config isn't recording all resource types that correspond to this control. Turn on recording for the following resources: AWS::IAM::User. To see all missing resource types for every enabled control, check your Config.1 findings.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: ELB target groups should use encrypted transport protocols
Findings.Description: This control checks whether an Elastic Load Balancing target group uses an encrypted transport protocol. This control does not apply to target groups with a target type of Lambda or ALB, or target groups using the GENEVE protocol. The control fails if the target group does not use HTTPS, TLS, or QUIC protocol.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: Application and Network Load Balancer target groups should use encrypted health check protocols
Findings.Description: This control checks whether the target group for application and network load balancer health checks use an encrypted transport protocol. The control fails if the health check protocol does not use HTTPS. This control is not applicable to Lambda target types.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: GuardDuty RDS Protection should be enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether GuardDuty RDS Protection is enabled. For a standalone account, the control fails if GuardDuty RDS Protection is disabled in the account. In a multi-account environment, the control fails if the delegated GuardDuty administrator account and all member accounts don't have RDS Protection enabled.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: Classic Load Balancers with SSL/HTTPS listeners should use a certificate provided by AWS Certificate Manager
Findings.Description: This control checks whether a Classic Load Balancer uses HTTPS/SSL certificates provided by AWS Certificate Manager. The control fails if a Classic Load Balancer that is configured with an HTTPS/SSL listener does not use a certificate provided by AWS Certificate Manager.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards
Findings.Title: Application and Network Load Balancer listeners should use secure protocols to encrypt data in transit
Findings.Description: This control checks whether the listener for an Application Load Balancer or Network Load Balancer is configured to use a secure protocol for encryption of data in transit. The control fails if an Application Load Balancer listener isn't configured to use the HTTPS protocol, or a Network Load Balancer listener isn't configured to use the TLS protocol.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: GuardDuty Malware Protection for EC2 should be enabled
Findings.Description: This control checks whether GuardDuty Malware Protection for EC2 is enabled. For a standalone account, the control fails if GuardDuty Malware Protection for EC2 is disabled in the account. In a multi-account environment, the control fails if the delegated GuardDuty administrator account and all member accounts don't have Malware Protection for EC2 enabled.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: Application and Network Load Balancers with listeners should use recommended security policies
Findings.Description: This control checks whether the HTTPS listener for an Application Load Balancer or the TLS listener for a Network Load Balancer is configured to encrypt data in transit by using a recommended security policy. The control fails if the HTTPS or TLS listener for a load balancer isn't configured to use a recommended security policy.

Recommendation.Text: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

Findings.Title: Classic Load Balancer should be configured with defensive or strictest desync mitigation mode
Findings.Description: This control checks whether the Classic Load Balancer is configured with defensive or strictest desync mitigation mode. This control will fail if the Classic Load Balancer is not configured with defensive or strictest desync mitigation mode.
